helpful: Provisioned petition text — the QP, split taxonomy, and vehicle argument drove the whole analysis
statpack per-Term paid/IFP grant-rate split (statpack.json classes) — the right anchor for a paid petition
fedcourts query on recent grants — distribution_count on prior grants corroborated the relist gradient
gaps: The predict prompt's 'Segment base rate by salience band' table is absent from the committed statpack.md/statpack.json — anchored on the paid-class rate instead
No corpus cut for 'response waived, no CFR' petitions — P(call-for-response) after a waiver had to be estimated from priors in training rather than data
A query filter for QP/legal-topic similarity on SCOTUS rows would beat the court/era/disposition filters for finding doctrinally similar cert priors
notes: Forward cell, clean provisioning. CourtListener carries no docket for this petition or its Vide companions (25-7403, 25-7477), so companion-petition status could not be checked; the supremecourt.gov snapshot was the sole docket source.
